papers.title: A modern Green Revolution gene for reduced height in wheat
papers.authors: Würschum T, Langer SM, Longin CFH, Tucker MR, Leiser WL

papers.title: Green Revolution dwarfing Rht genes negatively affected wheat floral traits related to cross-pollination efficiency
papers.authors: Schierenbeck M, Alqudah AM, Lantos E, Avogadro EG, Simón MR, Börner A

papers.title: Towards the replacement of wheat Green Revolution genes

edges.mechanism: Rht24 on chromosome 6A explains 15% of genotypic variance; allele substitution -8.8 cm; GA-sensitive
edges.evidence_sentence: Rht24 was the second most important locus for reduced height, explaining 15.0% of the genotypic variance and exerting an allele substitution effect of -8.8 cm

edges.mechanism: Dwarfing alleles reduce anther length up to 20% in Rht1+Rht2 combinations
edges.evidence_sentence: Decreases up to 20.2% in anther length were documented with the double gene combination

edges.mechanism: Dwarfing reduces filament length up to 33%
edges.evidence_sentence: 33% in filament length reductions relative to control plants

edges.mechanism: Rht-B1b/Rht-D1b shortens coleoptile (GA-insensitive dwarfs); GA-responsive Rht do NOT shorten coleoptile
edges.evidence_sentence: no shortening of the coleoptile in plants carrying the Rht18 allele, contrasting sharply with the coleoptile reduction observed in Rht-D1b lines

edges.mechanism: TEOSINTE BRANCHED 1 regulates plant height and stem internode length
edges.evidence_sentence: TEOSINTE BRANCHED 1 | Regulates height and stem internode length

perturbations.evidence_sentence: The Rht1+2 combination produced reductions up to 64% compared to the tall variety
perturbations.notes: Rht1+Rht2 double dwarf; up to 64% height reduction

perturbations.evidence_sentence: exerting an allele substitution effect of -8.8 cm
perturbations.notes: Rht24 allele substitution reduces height 8.8 cm
